user.title: Connect to Ampaera
user.description: Enter your API key from Settings > API in the Ampaera web app. Leave API URL empty to use production ({default_url}).

location.title: Configure Site
location.description:
Give this site a name and pick your **Nord Pool grid region** so spot prices match your area:

- **NO1** — Oslo & Eastern Norway
- **NO2** — Kristiansand & Southern Norway
- **NO3** — Trondheim & Central Norway
- **NO4** — Tromsø & Northern Norway
- **NO5** — Bergen & Western Norway

The site name is just a label (e.g. "Home", "Cabin") — pick whatever helps you tell sites apart in the Ampaera app.

data_description.site_name: A friendly name for this Home Assistant instance (shown in the Ampaera app).
data_description.grid_region: Determines which Nord Pool zone your hourly spot prices come from.

devices.title: Select Devices
devices.description:
Pick which Home Assistant devices Ampaera should sync.

**Found {total_count} devices in Home Assistant** — showing **{device_count}** for selection:
- {energy_count} look energy-relevant ({hidden_count} non-energy devices hidden — cameras, HA add-ons, notifications, …)
- {group_count} groups collapse {grouped_device_count} similar devices (e.g. multi-channel meters like em16 with 18 circuits)

**Recommended** power meters, EV chargers and water heaters are pre-selected. Untick anything you'd rather leave out, then submit. You can change this later under **Options → Manage Devices**.

data_description.selected_entities: Items marked *(grouped)* represent multi-channel meters — selecting one enables all its channels at once.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate Ampaera
reauth_confirm.description: Your API key has expired. Please enter a new API key.

simulation.title: Household Simulation (Optional)
simulation.description: Enable realistic household power simulation for demos and testing. This creates simulated appliances (dishwasher, oven, washing machine, etc.) that follow Norwegian daily patterns without needing the Ampaera backend.

error.no_devices_found: No compatible devices found in Home Assistant
error.no_devices_selected: Please select at least one device

abort.already_configured: This Home Assistant instance is already configured

reauth.title: Re-authenticate
reauth.description: Choose how to re-authenticate with Ampaera.

reauth_api_key.title: Enter New API Key
reauth_api_key.description: Enter a new API key to re-authenticate with Ampaera.

manage_devices.title: Manage Devices
manage_devices.description:
Select which devices Ampaera should sync.

**{device_count} of {total_count} devices shown** — {hidden_count} non-energy devices hidden, {group_count} groups collapse {grouped_device_count} similar devices.

Tick **Show all devices** below to list every channel individually.

data_description.selected_entities: Pre-selected items are Ampaera's recommendation. Untick anything you'd rather not sync.
data_description.show_all_devices: Reveals every channel inside grouped meters and includes non-energy devices.

settings.title: Ampaera Settings
settings.description: Configure integration behavior, simulation, and synchronization settings.

regenerate_dashboard.title: Regenerate Dashboard
regenerate_dashboard.description:
This will delete and recreate the Ampæra dashboard with the latest entity IDs. Use this if the dashboard shows 'Entity not found' errors after upgrading the integration.

**Click Submit to regenerate the dashboard.**

abort.reauth_oauth: OAuth re-authentication required. Please remove and re-add the integration to refresh OAuth credentials.
abort.dashboard_regenerated: Dashboard regenerated successfully! Refresh your browser to see the updated dashboard.

force_sync.name: Force device sync
force_sync.description: Immediately synchronize all devices with Ampaera cloud.

push_telemetry.name: Push telemetry
push_telemetry.description: Immediately push current telemetry to Ampaera cloud.
